quantum-developer.title: Foundations of Quantum Computing
quantum-developer.description: Understand the mathematical and physical principles that underpin quantum computing. Learn how qubits, superposition, and entanglement differ from classical bits.

resources.label: Qiskit Textbook - The Atoms of Computation

quantum-developer.challenge: Implement a Python script that represents a qubit state vector and manually applies a Hadamard transformation.

quantum-developer.title: Qiskit Setup and First Circuits
quantum-developer.description: Install and configure Qiskit and connect to IBM Q simulators. Build and run your first quantum circuits using basic gates.
quantum-developer.challenge: Build a 2-qubit circuit that creates a Bell state and verify the output probabilities using the statevector simulator.

quantum-developer.title: Quantum Gates and Unitary Operations
quantum-developer.description: Master single- and multi-qubit gate operations and understand their matrix representations. Learn how to compose gates to form arbitrary unitary transformations.

resources.label: Nielsen and Chuang - Quantum Computation and Quantum Information

quantum-developer.challenge: Decompose a given 2x2 unitary matrix into a sequence of standard Qiskit gates and verify correctness with unitary simulator.

quantum-developer.title: Quantum Measurement and Probability
quantum-developer.description: Understand the Born rule, projective measurement, and how measurement collapses quantum states. Learn to interpret shot-based results from real and simulated hardware.
quantum-developer.challenge: Run a GHZ-state circuit 1000 shots on QASM simulator and plot the resulting probability distribution, comparing to theoretical values.

quantum-developer.title: Quantum Algorithms Primer
quantum-developer.description: Survey the landscape of foundational quantum algorithms and their computational advantages. Implement Deutsch-Jozsa and Bernstein-Vazirani algorithms in Qiskit.
quantum-developer.challenge: Implement Bernstein-Vazirani for a hidden string of length 8 in Qiskit and confirm single-query recovery of the secret.

quantum-developer.title: Variational Quantum Circuits
quantum-developer.description: Learn how parameterized quantum circuits are used in variational algorithms for optimization and machine learning. Build ansatz circuits and optimize parameters using classical optimizers.
quantum-developer.challenge: Implement a 4-qubit VQE to find the ground-state energy of the H2 molecule using Qiskit Nature and a UCCSD ansatz.

quantum-developer.title: Quantum Error and Noise
quantum-developer.description: Understand the sources of noise in quantum hardware and how to model them in simulation. Apply basic error mitigation strategies to improve output fidelity.

resources.label: Quantum Error Correction - Devitt et al.

quantum-developer.challenge: Simulate a 3-qubit GHZ circuit under depolarizing noise and apply measurement error mitigation, reporting fidelity improvement.

quantum-developer.title: Running on IBM Quantum Hardware
quantum-developer.description: Submit quantum jobs to real IBM Quantum devices using Qiskit Runtime. Understand transpilation, qubit mapping, and how device topology affects circuit performance.
quantum-developer.challenge: Transpile and execute a 5-qubit circuit on a real IBM Quantum backend, then compare results with noise-model simulation.

quantum-developer.title: Quantum Software Engineering Patterns
quantum-developer.description: Apply software engineering best practices to quantum programs including testing, modular design, and reproducibility. Build reusable quantum circuit libraries and integrate them into classical pipelines.
quantum-developer.challenge: Build a parameterized quantum circuit library with unit tests and a benchmarking harness that tracks fidelity across Qiskit versions.

quantum-developer.title: End-to-End Quantum Application Development
quantum-developer.description: Design and deploy a full quantum-classical application that solves a real-world problem using IBM Quantum hardware. Integrate quantum subroutines into production-quality software architecture.
quantum-developer.challenge: Build and deploy a quantum portfolio optimization application using QAOA on IBM Quantum hardware with a REST API front-end.

quantum-algorithms.title: Mathematical Prerequisites for Quantum Algorithms
quantum-algorithms.description: Establish the mathematical foundation required to understand and analyze quantum algorithms. Cover vector spaces, tensor products, and computational complexity basics.
quantum-algorithms.challenge: Implement matrix exponentiation and tensor product operations in NumPy, then verify that Hadamard tensor Hadamard equals H2.

quantum-algorithms.title: Query Complexity and Oracle Models
quantum-algorithms.description: Understand the oracle model of computation and how quantum algorithms achieve query complexity advantages. Implement simple oracle-based algorithms from scratch.
quantum-algorithms.challenge: Prove the query complexity of Deutsch-Jozsa classically is O(2^n/2) worst case and implement both classical and quantum solvers for comparison.

quantum-algorithms.title: Quantum Fourier Transform
quantum-algorithms.description: Derive and implement the Quantum Fourier Transform and understand its role as a subroutine in major quantum algorithms. Analyze its exponential speedup over the classical FFT.
quantum-algorithms.challenge: Implement QFT and inverse QFT for 4 qubits in Qiskit without using the built-in library, and verify by recovering input state.

quantum-algorithms.title: Shor's Factoring Algorithm
quantum-algorithms.description: Understand the mathematical structure of Shor's algorithm and how order-finding via QFT enables integer factorization in polynomial time. Implement a small-scale version end to end.
quantum-algorithms.challenge: Implement Shor's algorithm from scratch to factor 15 in Qiskit, using full QFT and modular exponentiation, and explain each step.

quantum-algorithms.title: Grover's Search Algorithm
quantum-algorithms.description: Derive Grover's amplitude amplification algorithm and analyze its quadratic speedup for unstructured search. Implement it for database search and satisfiability problems.
quantum-algorithms.challenge: Implement Grover search on a 6-qubit space with a 2-solution oracle and verify quadratic speedup in query count versus brute force.

quantum-algorithms.title: Quantum Walk Algorithms
quantum-algorithms.description: Study discrete and continuous quantum walks and their algorithmic applications including element distinctness and triangle finding. Compare quantum walk speedups to classical random walks.
quantum-algorithms.challenge: Implement a discrete quantum walk on a cycle graph in NumPy and compare the probability distribution spread to a classical random walk after 50 steps.

quantum-algorithms.title: HHL and Quantum Linear Systems
quantum-algorithms.description: Study the Harrow-Hassidim-Lloyd algorithm for solving linear systems exponentially faster than classical methods under specific conditions. Analyze its caveats and practical limitations.

resources.label: HHL Original Paper - Harrow et al.

quantum-algorithms.challenge: Implement HHL for a 2x2 sparse matrix in Qiskit and compare the quantum solution vector with the classical NumPy linalg.solve output.

quantum-algorithms.title: Variational and Hybrid Algorithms
quantum-algorithms.description: Study NISQ-era variational algorithms including VQE and QAOA and understand their optimization landscapes. Analyze when variational approaches can outperform classical heuristics.

resources.label: VQE Original Paper - Peruzzo et al.
QAOA Original Paper - Farhi et al.

quantum-algorithms.challenge: Compare VQE with RY ansatz vs UCCSD for H2 ground state energy and report convergence speed and final accuracy differences.

quantum-algorithms.title: Quantum Simulation Algorithms
quantum-algorithms.description: Study Hamiltonian simulation algorithms including Trotter-Suzuki decompositions and qubitization. Understand how quantum simulation enables exponential speedup for physics problems.
quantum-algorithms.challenge: Implement first and second-order Trotter simulation for a transverse-field Ising model on 4 qubits and measure error vs step size.

quantum-algorithms.title: Fault-Tolerant Algorithm Design and Resource Estimation
quantum-algorithms.description: Analyze quantum algorithms in the fault-tolerant setting and produce rigorous resource estimates for T-gate and qubit counts. Understand which algorithms are viable on future fault-tolerant hardware.

resources.label: T-gate Estimation for Shor and Grover

quantum-algorithms.challenge: Produce a full T-gate and logical qubit resource estimate for running Shor's algorithm to factor a 2048-bit RSA key using the Azure resource estimator.

quantum-ml.title: Classical ML and Quantum Computing Basics
quantum-ml.description: Review classical machine learning fundamentals and understand how quantum computing concepts map onto ML workflows. Set up PennyLane and run your first quantum node.

resources.label: Quantum Machine Learning - Biamonte et al.

quantum-ml.challenge: Build a PennyLane QNode that takes a classical 2D input, encodes it as rotation angles, and outputs a single expectation value.

quantum-ml.title: Data Encoding Strategies
quantum-ml.description: Study the main methods for encoding classical data into quantum states, including amplitude, angle, and basis encodings. Understand the tradeoffs in expressiveness and circuit depth.
quantum-ml.challenge: Implement all four encoding schemes for the Iris dataset in PennyLane and compare the resulting Hilbert space inner products.

quantum-ml.title: Variational Quantum Classifiers
quantum-ml.description: Build variational quantum classifiers by combining parameterized quantum circuits with classical optimizers. Train a VQC on a binary classification task and evaluate accuracy.
quantum-ml.challenge: Train a VQC on the moons dataset using PennyLane + PyTorch and achieve at least 90% test accuracy, logging loss per epoch.

quantum-ml.title: Quantum Kernels and Support Vector Machines
quantum-ml.description: Understand quantum kernel methods where quantum circuits compute inner products in exponentially large feature spaces. Train a quantum kernel SVM and compare to classical RBF kernels.

resources.label: Quantum Kernel Machine Learning - Havlicek et al.

quantum-ml.challenge: Implement a quantum kernel SVM on the breast cancer dataset using Qiskit ML and report classification accuracy vs a classical RBF SVM.

quantum-ml.title: Quantum Neural Networks
quantum-ml.description: Design quantum neural network architectures using alternating layers of encoding and variational circuits. Analyze expressibility and entangling power of different QNN designs.

resources.label: Expressibility of Quantum Circuits - Sim et al.

quantum-ml.challenge: Design and train a quantum convolutional neural network for MNIST binary classification and compare parameter count vs a classical CNN.

quantum-ml.title: Quantum Generative Models
quantum-ml.description: Study quantum generative adversarial networks and quantum Boltzmann machines for data generation tasks. Implement a QGAN to generate synthetic data distributions.
quantum-ml.challenge: Train a QGAN to reproduce a bimodal 1D Gaussian mixture and evaluate using KL divergence between generated and target distributions.

quantum-ml.title: Quantum Natural Language Processing
quantum-ml.description: Explore quantum approaches to NLP including DisCoCat compositional models and quantum sentence classification. Implement a small quantum text classifier.
quantum-ml.challenge: Use lambeq to classify 50 sentences into two categories using a quantum circuit model and report F1 score.

quantum-ml.title: Quantum Transfer Learning
quantum-ml.description: Apply transfer learning techniques to quantum ML by combining pretrained classical networks with trainable quantum layers. Fine-tune hybrid models on small datasets.

resources.label: Transfer Learning in QML - Mari et al.

quantum-ml.challenge: Attach a 4-qubit PennyLane layer to a pretrained ResNet-18 and fine-tune on 200 images from a 4-class subset of CIFAR-10.

quantum-ml.title: Quantum ML on Real Hardware
quantum-ml.description: Deploy quantum ML models onto real quantum hardware and analyze the effect of noise on model performance. Apply noise-aware training and error mitigation techniques.

resources.label: Noise in QML - Wang et al.

quantum-ml.challenge: Train a VQC classifier on a simulator then run inference on IBM Quantum hardware, and quantify accuracy degradation due to hardware noise.

quantum-ml.title: Quantum Advantage in ML and Research Frontiers
quantum-ml.description: Critically evaluate claimed quantum advantages in ML, study dequantization results, and explore open research problems. Reproduce a recent QML paper and propose an improvement.

resources.label: QML in the NISQ Era - Cerezo et al.

quantum-ml.challenge: Reproduce the main classification experiment from a 2023 QML paper using PennyLane, then propose and test one architectural modification.

quantum-crypto.title: Classical Cryptography and Quantum Threats
quantum-crypto.description: Review the classical cryptographic primitives that quantum computers will break and understand why they are vulnerable. Survey the landscape of post-quantum cryptographic approaches.
topics: Shor's threat to public key crypto

resources.label: Introduction to Modern Cryptography - Katz and Lindell

quantum-crypto.challenge: Write a report mapping each NIST PQC finalist algorithm to the hardness assumption it relies on, with a one-paragraph explanation of each.

quantum-crypto.title: BB84 Quantum Key Distribution
quantum-crypto.description: Understand the BB84 protocol and the physical principles that make quantum key distribution unconditionally secure. Implement a simulation of BB84 including eavesdropper detection.

resources.label: BB84 Original Paper - Bennett and Brassard

quantum-crypto.challenge: Implement a full BB84 simulation in Python including an eavesdropper inserting herself on 50% of qubits and computing the resulting QBER.

quantum-crypto.title: Advanced QKD Protocols
quantum-crypto.description: Study E91, B92, and continuous-variable QKD protocols and compare their practical advantages. Implement E91 using entangled pairs and Bell inequality verification.
quantum-crypto.challenge: Implement the E91 protocol in Qiskit, verify Bell inequality violation to confirm entanglement, and show how eavesdropping reduces CHSH score.

quantum-crypto.title: Lattice-Based Cryptography
quantum-crypto.description: Study the hardness of lattice problems SVP and LWE and how they underpin post-quantum cryptographic schemes. Implement basic lattice operations and understand CRYSTALS-Kyber.

resources.label: A Decade of Lattice Cryptography - Peikert

quantum-crypto.challenge: Implement the LWE key encapsulation mechanism from scratch in Python for small parameters and verify encrypt-decrypt roundtrip correctness.

quantum-crypto.title: Hash-Based and Code-Based Signatures
quantum-crypto.description: Study hash-based signature schemes SPHINCS+ and XMSS and code-based cryptography including Classic McEliece. Implement a Merkle tree authentication scheme.
quantum-crypto.challenge: Implement a Merkle tree one-time signature scheme in Python, sign 16 messages, and verify that signature reuse is detectable.

quantum-crypto.title: Quantum Random Number Generation
quantum-crypto.description: Understand how quantum mechanics provides true randomness and how QRNGs differ from pseudo-random generators. Implement a simple QRNG using measurement outcomes.
quantum-crypto.challenge: Build a QRNG using Qiskit on IBM hardware, collect 10000 bits, and apply NIST randomness test suite to verify statistical quality.

quantum-crypto.title: Quantum Authentication and Secret Sharing
quantum-crypto.description: Study quantum authentication protocols and quantum secret sharing schemes that use entanglement for information-theoretic security. Implement a (2,3) quantum threshold secret sharing scheme.

resources.label: Quantum Secret Sharing - Hillery et al.

quantum-crypto.challenge: Implement a (2,3) quantum secret sharing protocol in Qiskit using GHZ states and verify that any 2 of 3 parties can reconstruct the secret.

quantum-crypto.title: Post-Quantum TLS and Protocol Migration
quantum-crypto.description: Study how TLS and other internet protocols are being updated to use post-quantum algorithms. Implement a hybrid classical/post-quantum handshake using liboqs.
quantum-crypto.challenge: Set up an OQS-enabled TLS server using liboqs with Kyber key exchange, capture the handshake, and verify the PQ algorithm was negotiated.

quantum-crypto.title: Security Proofs in Quantum Settings
quantum-crypto.description: Study formal security models for quantum adversaries including the quantum random oracle model and post-quantum security reductions. Understand how QROM proofs differ from classical ROM proofs.
quantum-crypto.challenge: Write out the QROM security proof for OAEP-Kyber in pseudocode, identifying where classical and quantum adversary steps differ.

quantum-crypto.title: Quantum Cryptography Research and Deployment
quantum-crypto.description: Survey deployed QKD networks and the current state of post-quantum cryptography rollout in industry. Design a cryptographic migration plan for a hypothetical enterprise system.
quantum-crypto.challenge: Design a complete post-quantum cryptographic migration roadmap for a 500-person company, including certificate rotation, protocol upgrades, and 5-year timeline.

quantum-researcher.title: Quantum Mechanics for Quantum Computing Research
quantum-researcher.description: Build a rigorous mathematical foundation in quantum mechanics as it applies to quantum information science. Study Hilbert spaces, operators, and the postulates of quantum mechanics at a graduate level.
quantum-researcher.challenge: Derive the Kraus operator representation for a depolarizing channel and verify trace preservation and complete positivity properties numerically.

quantum-researcher.title: Quantum Information Theory
quantum-researcher.description: Study the theory of quantum information including entropy measures, quantum channel capacity, and entanglement measures. Implement von Neumann entropy and mutual information calculations.
quantum-researcher.challenge: Write a Python function that computes the entanglement of formation for an arbitrary 2-qubit mixed state using the Wootters formula.

quantum-researcher.title: Quantum Error Correction Theory
quantum-researcher.description: Study the theory of quantum error correcting codes including stabilizer formalism and the threshold theorem. Implement the 3-qubit bit-flip and Shor codes from scratch.
quantum-researcher.challenge: Implement the 5-qubit perfect code in Cirq, inject a single-qubit error on each qubit position, and verify syndrome detection and correction.

quantum-researcher.title: Hamiltonian Engineering and Quantum Control
quantum-researcher.description: Study Hamiltonian engineering techniques for quantum computing including pulse-level control and the Magnus expansion. Simulate time-dependent quantum systems.
quantum-researcher.challenge: Use QuTiP and GRAPE to design a pulse sequence implementing a CNOT gate on a two-qubit system and compare fidelity to the Rabi-frequency baseline.

quantum-researcher.title: Surface Codes and Topological Error Correction
quantum-researcher.description: Study the surface code and toric code in depth, including syndrome extraction, minimum-weight perfect matching decoding, and logical qubit operations. Simulate surface code cycles.
quantum-researcher.challenge: Simulate a distance-3 surface code using Stim, introduce random Pauli errors at various rates, and plot the logical error rate vs physical error rate curve.

quantum-researcher.title: Q# and Quantum Program Verification
quantum-researcher.description: Write quantum programs in Q# and use the Microsoft Quantum Development Kit for resource estimation and verification. Develop formally verified quantum subroutines.
quantum-researcher.challenge: Implement Shor's algorithm in Q# using native library subroutines and run the Azure Resource Estimator to get T-gate and qubit counts for N=2048.

quantum-researcher.title: Quantum Complexity and Computational Models
quantum-researcher.description: Study the quantum computational complexity hierarchy including BQP, QMA, and QCMA. Understand the limits of quantum speedup and the role of quantum proofs.
quantum-researcher.challenge: Formally prove that the local Hamiltonian problem is QMA-complete by sketching both the QMA membership and QMA-hardness reductions.

quantum-researcher.title: Quantum Communication and Nonlocality
quantum-researcher.description: Study quantum communication protocols, Bell inequalities, and the role of entanglement in distributed quantum information processing. Implement CHSH and other Bell tests.

resources.label: Bell Nonlocality Review - Brunner et al.
Quantum Information and Quantum Computation - Bennett

quantum-researcher.challenge: Implement the CHSH game in Cirq, simulate both classical and quantum strategies, and verify that the quantum strategy achieves a winning probability above the classical bound.

quantum-researcher.title: Open Quantum Systems and Decoherence
quantum-researcher.description: Study the Lindblad master equation, Markovian dynamics, and decoherence mechanisms in real quantum hardware. Simulate open quantum system dynamics using QuTiP.
topics: T1 and T2 processes in Lindblad form

resources.label: Open Quantum Systems - Breuer and Petruccione

quantum-researcher.challenge: Simulate amplitude damping and dephasing on a single qubit using QuTiP Lindblad solver and plot T1 and T2 decay curves, fitting to exponentials.

quantum-researcher.title: Research Frontiers and Original Contribution
quantum-researcher.description: Engage with the current research literature, identify an open problem, and produce an original research contribution. Learn to write and review quantum computing research papers.

resources.label: How to Write a Quantum Computing Paper

quantum-researcher.challenge: Identify an open problem from a 2024 quant-ph paper, formulate a conjecture, and write a 4-page research proposal with preliminary numerical evidence.

quantum-hardware.title: Physics of Quantum Hardware Platforms
quantum-hardware.description: Survey the major physical platforms for quantum computing including superconducting qubits and trapped ions. Understand the key figures of merit for each platform.

resources.label: Quantum Computing Hardware Review - Krantz et al.

quantum-hardware.challenge: Create a comparison table of superconducting vs trapped ion platforms across 8 metrics including coherence time, gate fidelity, and scalability.

quantum-hardware.title: Superconducting Qubit Circuit Theory
quantum-hardware.description: Study the circuit quantum electrodynamics framework for superconducting qubits. Understand Josephson junctions, LC oscillators, and the transmon qubit Hamiltonian.
quantum-hardware.challenge: Derive the transmon Hamiltonian from the Cooper pair box model using the Josephson and charging energy ratio EJ/EC and plot the energy levels as EJ/EC increases.

quantum-hardware.title: Quantum Gate Implementation and Pulse Design
quantum-hardware.description: Learn how quantum gates are physically implemented as microwave pulses on superconducting hardware. Design and simulate gate pulses using Qiskit Pulse.
quantum-hardware.challenge: Design a DRAG-shaped pi pulse in Qiskit Pulse for a transmon qubit, simulate it with noise, and compare leakage to a simple Gaussian pulse.

quantum-hardware.title: Qubit Readout and State Discrimination
quantum-hardware.description: Study dispersive readout of superconducting qubits and the signal processing chain for state discrimination. Implement IQ plane classification and calibration.
quantum-hardware.challenge: Acquire IQ readout data from IBM Quantum backend, fit Gaussian clusters per state, and implement a linear discriminant classifier to maximize readout fidelity.

quantum-hardware.title: Quantum Characterization and Benchmarking
quantum-hardware.description: Master standard characterization protocols for quantum hardware including process tomography and randomized benchmarking. Quantify gate fidelities and coherence times.
quantum-hardware.challenge: Run T1, T2, and randomized benchmarking experiments on an IBM Quantum backend using Qiskit Experiments and produce a hardware characterization report.

quantum-hardware.title: Crosstalk and Hardware Error Characterization
quantum-hardware.description: Study crosstalk between qubits on multi-qubit chips and methods for characterizing and mitigating it. Understand leakage to non-computational states and its effects.
quantum-hardware.challenge: Design a Hamiltonian tomography experiment for two coupled qubits to extract the ZZ coupling strength and verify by comparing to independent T2 measurements.

quantum-hardware.title: Trapped Ion Hardware Engineering
quantum-hardware.description: Study the engineering of trapped ion quantum computers including Paul traps, laser interactions, and motional mode gates. Compare engineering challenges to superconducting platforms.

resources.label: Trapped Ion Quantum Computing - Bruzewicz et al.
Quantum Computing with Ions - Leibfried et al.

quantum-hardware.challenge: Simulate a Molmer-Sorensen gate on two ions in a harmonic trap using QuTiP, sweeping the Lamb-Dicke parameter and plotting entanglement fidelity.

quantum-hardware.title: Quantum Hardware Calibration Pipelines
quantum-hardware.description: Design automated calibration pipelines for quantum hardware including drift tracking and closed-loop optimization. Understand the workflow used in production quantum systems.
quantum-hardware.challenge: Implement an automated calibration pipeline in Python that runs T1, T2, and Rabi experiments sequentially, updates gate parameters, and logs results.

quantum-hardware.title: Cryogenic Engineering and Hardware Integration
quantum-hardware.description: Study the cryogenic infrastructure required for superconducting quantum computers and the full signal chain from room-temperature electronics to the quantum chip. Understand thermalization and shielding.
quantum-hardware.challenge: Design a complete wiring schematic for a 5-qubit superconducting processor including attenuation, filtering, and amplification at each cryostat stage.

quantum-hardware.title: Scaling Quantum Hardware and Next-Generation Architectures
quantum-hardware.description: Study the engineering challenges and proposed solutions for scaling quantum hardware to fault-tolerant regimes. Analyze modular architectures, 3D integration, and photonic interconnects.
quantum-hardware.challenge: Design a modular 1000-qubit architecture proposal including inter-module connectivity, error correction overhead, and estimated clock cycle performance.

quantum-optimization.title: Classical Optimization Foundations
quantum-optimization.description: Review classical combinatorial and continuous optimization to understand the problems that quantum approaches target. Study NP-hard problems and heuristic methods.
quantum-optimization.challenge: Implement simulated annealing for Max-Cut on a 20-node random 3-regular graph and record solution quality vs runtime for 5 different cooling schedules.

quantum-optimization.title: Ising Model and QUBO Formulations
quantum-optimization.description: Learn to formulate combinatorial optimization problems as Ising models and Quadratic Unconstrained Binary Optimization problems. Convert real-world problems to QUBO form.

resources.label: QUBO Formulations Survey - Glover et al.

quantum-optimization.challenge: Formulate a 10-city TSP as a QUBO matrix in Python, print the full penalty and objective terms, and verify the QUBO minimum corresponds to a valid tour.

quantum-optimization.title: Quantum Annealing and D-Wave Systems
quantum-optimization.description: Understand quantum annealing theory and how D-Wave machines implement it using transverse-field Ising Hamiltonians. Submit real jobs to D-Wave Leap.

resources.label: Quantum Annealing Overview - Johnson et al.

quantum-optimization.challenge: Submit a Max-Cut problem on a 64-node graph to D-Wave Leap using Ocean SDK and compare the best annealing solution to the simulated annealing baseline.

quantum-optimization.title: QAOA Theory and Implementation
quantum-optimization.description: Derive the Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm from first principles and implement it for Max-Cut. Analyze the role of circuit depth p and the approximation ratio.

resources.label: QAOA Original Paper - Farhi et al.

quantum-optimization.challenge: Implement QAOA at depths p=1, 2, and 3 for Max-Cut on a 6-node graph and plot the approximation ratio improvement vs number of CNOT gates used.

quantum-optimization.title: QAOA Variants and Mixer Designs
quantum-optimization.description: Study advanced QAOA variants including QAOA with Grover mixers, constrained QAOA, and recursive QAOA. Implement a constraint-preserving mixer for a knapsack problem.
quantum-optimization.challenge: Implement QAOA with an XY mixer for the maximum independent set problem on a 8-node graph and verify feasibility of all returned solutions.

quantum-optimization.title: Variational Quantum Optimization Beyond QAOA
quantum-optimization.description: Explore non-QAOA variational optimization strategies including QITE, FALQON, and counterdiabatic driving. Compare convergence and hardware requirements.
quantum-optimization.challenge: Implement FALQON for Max-Cut on a 6-node graph in Qiskit and compare convergence speed and final cut value to QAOA at equivalent circuit depth.

quantum-optimization.title: Quantum Optimization for Finance
quantum-optimization.description: Apply quantum optimization algorithms to financial problems including portfolio selection, risk management, and options pricing. Formulate and solve a portfolio optimization with constraints.
quantum-optimization.challenge: Use QAOA via Qiskit Finance to find the minimum-variance portfolio for 6 assets with a budget constraint and compare to the classical efficient frontier.

quantum-optimization.title: Quantum Optimization for Logistics
quantum-optimization.description: Solve logistics optimization problems including vehicle routing, scheduling, and supply chain using quantum annealing and gate-based methods. Formulate VRP as a QUBO.

resources.label: Quantum Optimization for Logistics - Feld et al.

quantum-optimization.challenge: Formulate a 5-vehicle 20-customer VRP as a QUBO, submit to D-Wave with minor embedding, and compare route quality to Google OR-Tools classical solution.

quantum-optimization.title: Quantum Optimization Benchmarking and Limits
quantum-optimization.description: Critically evaluate claims of quantum optimization advantage using rigorous benchmarking methodology. Understand when quantum optimization may or may not outperform classical methods.

resources.label: No Quantum Speedup for D-Wave - Ronnow et al.

quantum-optimization.challenge: Design and execute a benchmarking study comparing QAOA, D-Wave annealing, and Gurobi on 50 Max-Cut instances of varying size, plotting time-to-solution vs instance size.

quantum-optimization.title: Quantum Optimization Research and Applications
quantum-optimization.description: Engage with cutting-edge quantum optimization research and design a novel application for a real-world optimization problem. Produce an end-to-end demonstration with rigorous evaluation.
quantum-optimization.challenge: Identify a real industry optimization problem, formulate it as a QUBO, run QAOA and D-Wave solutions, and submit a 6-page technical report with reproducible benchmarks.

quantum-simulation.title: Quantum Simulation Fundamentals
quantum-simulation.description: Understand why quantum computers are naturally suited for simulating quantum systems and what computational advantage this provides. Survey simulation targets from chemistry to condensed matter.
topics: Types of quantum simulation - digital vs analog

resources.label: Quantum Simulation Review - Georgescu et al.

quantum-simulation.challenge: Compute the eigenspectrum of the 4x4 Heisenberg Hamiltonian classically in NumPy and estimate how computational cost scales with system size.

quantum-simulation.title: Second Quantization and Fermionic Systems
quantum-simulation.description: Learn the second quantization formalism for fermionic and bosonic systems and how to represent molecular Hamiltonians computationally. Map fermionic operators to qubit operators.
quantum-simulation.challenge: Use OpenFermion to construct the Jordan-Wigner qubit Hamiltonian for LiH at bond length 1.5 Angstrom and compare qubit operator term counts.

quantum-simulation.title: Variational Quantum Eigensolver
quantum-simulation.description: Implement the Variational Quantum Eigensolver algorithm to find molecular ground state energies. Study ansatz choices including hardware-efficient and chemically motivated UCC forms.

resources.label: VQE Original Paper - Peruzzo et al.

quantum-simulation.challenge: Run VQE for H2 and LiH ground state energies in Qiskit Nature using both UCCSD and hardware-efficient ansatz, and plot energy vs optimizer iteration for each.

quantum-simulation.title: Hamiltonian Simulation Methods
quantum-simulation.description: Study digital Hamiltonian simulation methods including Trotter decomposition, LCU, and quantum signal processing. Implement and compare error bounds.

resources.label: Hamiltonian Simulation Methods - Childs et al.

quantum-simulation.challenge: Implement 1st and 2nd order Trotter for the transverse-field Ising model and plot simulation error versus Trotter step size on a log-log scale.

quantum-simulation.title: Quantum Phase Estimation for Simulation
quantum-simulation.description: Study quantum phase estimation as a method for extracting energy eigenvalues of molecular Hamiltonians with exponential precision. Implement QPE for a simple system.

resources.label: QPE for Chemistry - Babbush et al.

quantum-simulation.challenge: Implement QPE in Qiskit to compute the ground state energy of H2 using a 6-qubit ancilla register and compare precision to VQE on the same backend.

quantum-simulation.title: Excited States and Quantum Subspace Expansion
quantum-simulation.description: Extend VQE and quantum simulation to excited states using quantum subspace expansion, QEOM, and multistate methods. Simulate absorption spectra for small molecules.

resources.label: Quantum Subspace Expansion - McClean et al.

quantum-simulation.challenge: Use QSE in Qiskit Nature to compute the first 3 excited state energies of H2 and compare to the exact FCI spectrum.

quantum-simulation.title: Quantum Dynamics and Real-Time Simulation
quantum-simulation.description: Simulate real-time quantum dynamics of many-body systems including spin chains and molecular dynamics. Study McLachlan variational principle for time-dependent VQE.

resources.label: Variational Quantum Simulation - Yuan et al.

quantum-simulation.challenge: Simulate the real-time dynamics of a 6-site transverse Ising chain after a quench using TDVQE and compare magnetization dynamics to exact Trotter simulation.

quantum-simulation.title: Condensed Matter Quantum Simulation
quantum-simulation.description: Apply quantum simulation to condensed matter systems including Hubbard models and topological phases. Study signatures of quantum phase transitions on quantum hardware.
quantum-simulation.challenge: Simulate the 4-site Fermi-Hubbard chain as a function of U/t ratio using VQE and plot the double occupancy versus U/t to locate the Mott transition.

quantum-simulation.title: Noise-Aware Quantum Simulation
quantum-simulation.description: Develop strategies for quantum simulation in the presence of hardware noise, including error mitigation tailored to simulation workflows. Compare simulated and hardware results.

resources.label: Error Mitigation for Quantum Chemistry - Endo et al.

quantum-simulation.challenge: Run VQE for H2 on a real IBM Quantum backend with and without ZNE error mitigation and compare energy accuracy and variance to noiseless simulator.

quantum-simulation.title: Towards Quantum Advantage in Simulation
quantum-simulation.description: Survey the current state of quantum advantage in simulation, identify the near-term and fault-tolerant milestones, and propose a simulation experiment at the quantum advantage frontier.
quantum-simulation.challenge: Design a quantum simulation experiment targeting a classically intractable active space, estimate qubit and gate requirements, and identify the nearest IBM Quantum device capable of running it.
